'''Adam Swift''' (born 20 February 1993) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

He has played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from the [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] at [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in the [[2013 RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[2013 Championship 1|Championship 1]] and the [[2014 RFL Championship|Championship]]. Swift has also spent time away from St Helens on loan at the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]]. He won the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] with his hometown club St Helens.

==Background==
Swift was born and has lived in [[St Helens, Merseyside]], England all his life. In his secondary education, he attended [[Cowley International College]].

==Career==
===St Helens===
Swift made his [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] [[Super League]] début in 2012.

His number was 26 but for the 2014 season, he wore the number 5 as first choice winger.

Swift made some appearances for [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] as fullback on dual registration from [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].

St Helens reached the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]], and Swift was selected to play on the wing in their 14–6 victory over the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last1=Cartwright|first1=Phil|title=St Helens v Wigan as it happened|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/live/rugby-league/29416637|accessdate=12 October 2014|work=[[BBC Sport]]|publisher=[[BBC]]|date=11 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|accessdate=15 October 2014}}</ref>

===Hull FC===
In round 9 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Swift scored two tries for Hull F.C. in a 30–12 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12329593/super-league-adam-swift-and-mahe-fonua-set-hull-fc-on-course-for-30-12-win-over-castleford-tigers/|title= Adam Swift and Mahe Fonua set Hull FC on course for 30-12 win over Castleford Tigers|website=skysports.com|accessdate=16 March 2022}}</ref>
The following week, he scored another two tries as Hull F.C. defeated [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] 64–22.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57539303|title=Leigh Centurions 22-64 Hull FC - Savelio grabs treble in 12-try rout|work=BBC Sport |access-date=16 March 2022}}</ref>
Swift played a total of 24 games for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season and scored 19 tries as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Huddersfield===
On 24 September 2023 it was announced that Swift had signed with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] for the 2024 season on a three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|title=Adam Swift: Huddersfield Giants sign Hull FC winger for 2024 season|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66912101|date=25 Sep 2023}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Swift made his club debut for Huddersfield in their 16-8 victory over Leigh. In round 4, Swift scored two tries for Huddersfield in their 50-8 win against Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68501494|title=Super League: Castleford 8-50 Huddersfield - Tigers suffer fourth straight loss to nine-try Giants|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 7, Swift scored a [[hat-trick]] in the clubs 56-22 victory over Hull F.C.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match-reports/hull-fc-produce-horrific-defensive-9209610/|title=Hull FC produce horrific defensive showing as Huddersfield Giants thump helpless side|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>
Swift played 17 games for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest admission made over 2025 shortcomings|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
